name: Hawaiian Bagels
recipeIngredient: 5/8 cup dried coconut, shredded or flaked
recipeInstructions:
1. Place all ingredients in the order listed into the bread machine.
2. Add the pineapple and coconut 5 minutes before the end of the Kneading phase or work them into the dough before shaping.
3. Set the machine to the Dough setting, or stop it after the rise is complete.
4. Prepare baking sheets or use nonstick sheets.
5. Remove the dough from the machine, punch it down, and roll it out into a rectangle about 14 x 18 inches.
6. Divide the dough into equal pieces and shape them into bagel form or cut using a cutter. Place the bagels on the prepared sheets.
7. Let the bagels rise for another 20 minutes to 4 hours (or as long as needed).
8. Fill a 4 to 6-quart soup pot with water 3 to 4 inches deep. Optionally add 2 tablespoons of malt syrup, honey, sugar, or non-diastatic malt powder.
9. Bring the water to a boil.
10. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
11. Drop the bagels one at a time into the boiling water. Boil only 3 or 4 at a time so they don't crowd each other.
12. Simmer each side for about a minute, then return them to the baking sheet.
13. Add any glazes or toppings as desired.
14. Bake in the preheated oven just below the middle temperature (400°F/200°C) for 15 minutes on the first sheet and less time on subsequent sheets.
15. These are best enjoyed while still warm. They can be frozen; you may want to slice them first so they can be toasted in a toaster.
